name: Zadar Old Town (Sea Organ + Sun Salutation)
description: Trip starting point. Old town peninsula with the Sea Organ and Greeting to the Sun at sunset — Hitchcock called it the best sunset in the world.

name: Plitvice Lakes National Park
description: 16 terraced turquoise lakes with waterfalls. ~1.5h north of Zadar — only realistic as a day one/two detour before heading south.
bestTime: enter at 07:00 or after 16:00 to dodge tour buses
communityNotes: Plitvice rejected: +4h drive in wrong direction, swimming banned, 36pp boardwalk. Paklenica is the gorge fix on the route.

name: Krka National Park (Skradinski Buk)
description: Waterfall cascades between Zadar and Split. Swimming below the falls is no longer allowed, but the boardwalk loop is short and spectacular.
communityNotes: ⚠️ REJECTED: Swimming banned since 2021. €40pp for a boardwalk is bad value for this group. Kravica Falls (Bosnia, Day 6) delivers the waterfall swimming experience at €5-8pp. Recent 2024-2026 reviews confirm it's not worth it without swimming.

name: Paklenica National Park
description: Velebit canyon park ~45min from Zadar: serious hiking and one of Europe's best-known climbing spots. Velika Paklenica gorge walk to the mountain hut is the classic.

name: Telašćica Nature Park (Dugi Otok)
description: Cliffs, salt lake and bays on Dugi Otok island, reachable by ferry from Zadar. Candidate for the 'island day' if we take the car ferry.

name: Split (Diocletian's Palace)
description: Roman palace turned living old town. Also the main ferry hub for Hvar/Brač/Vis and good nightlife.

name: Omiš (Cetina canyon)
description: Pirate town where the Cetina river canyon meets the sea: ziplining over the canyon, rafting, canyoning, free-climbing, fortress viewpoints. Adventure-sports hub of the coast.

name: Biokovo Skywalk (Makarska)
description: Glass platform at ~1228m above the Makarska riviera; mountain road drive up Biokovo. Sea-and-islands panorama.

name: Hvar Town
description: Island town known for beaches, the Spanish fortress and the party scene (Hula Hula, Carpe Diem). Car ferry from Split (Stari Grad) or Drvenik (Sućuraj).

name: Dubrovnik Old Town
description: Trip end point — car drop-off and bus/flight back to Vienna on Jun 28. Walls walk, Srđ cable car/viewpoint, Buža cliff bar.
bestTime: early morning or evening — brutal cruise-ship crowds midday

name: Mostar (Stari Most)
description: Ottoman old town around the rebuilt 16th-century bridge; locals dive 24m into the Neretva. Main Bosnia base candidate.
bestTime: stay overnight — day-trip crowds leave by 18:00

name: Blagaj Tekija
description: Dervish monastery at the cliff spring of the Buna river, 20min from Mostar. Spring is one of the strongest karst springs in Europe. Knees/shoulders covered to enter the tekija (wraps provided free). Park at the lower lots — the final street is a midday tour-bus trap.
cost: 10 KM / €5 cash to enter the tekija

name: Kravica Waterfalls
description: 25m horseshoe waterfall arc on the Trebižat river — you can swim right below the falls. ~40min from Mostar, on the route to/from Croatia.
bestTime: arrive ~07:00–08:00 to beat the crowds; June pool swimmable (~18–20°C)

name: Počitelj
description: Ottoman hillside fortress village on the Neretva, basically on the Mostar–coast road. Quick climb to the tower for the view. Free 30-min stop if passing at golden hour.

name: Trebinje
description: Relaxed Herzegovinian wine town 30min inland from Dubrovnik — locals' favorite cheap base near Dubrovnik. Candidate for the last nights (much cheaper than Dubrovnik).

name: Dubrovnik Airport (Čilipi) — friend pickup + car drop-off
description: TWO HARD ANCHORS here (20km S of Dubrovnik, Konavle): (1) pick up the 4th person Jun 18 evening; (2) DROP THE RENTAL CAR by 20:00 on Jun 28 (Sicily By Car). Days 1–3 must end near here; the loop returns here at the end. Full day usable on Jun 28 before the 20:00 drop.

name: Zadar Airport (ZAD) — car pickup Jun 16 10:00
description: TRIP START: rental car (Kia Sportage or similar, Sicily By Car) ready at 10:00 on Jun 16, after arrival flight FR4807. 10km SE of Zadar old town. Day 1 effectively starts here ~10:30. See research/rental-car.md.

name: Sutjeska National Park (Tjentište)
description: Bosnia's oldest national park, on our exit route east towards the Šćepan Polje border: Maglić (BiH's highest peak), Perućica primeval forest, dramatic Tjentište valley with the brutalist WWII monument. Fits the 'deeper into the Bosnian mountains' wish on a transit day.
